title: Storage vs. Projection (The CQRS of Thought)
description: The storage of knowledge can and must be separate from its projection. A relational database is not the information it stores; it is merely a search index. The underlying truth (the event stream/ledger) can have infinite, simultaneous, imperfect projections. This concept bridges the gap between immutable algorithmic memory (the ledger, storing what actually happened or was stated) and fluid, reconstructive human-like recall (the materialized views, adapting information for immediate contextual use). As the adage goes: 'All models are wrong, some are useful.'
